name: Altman Z-Score
purpose: Estimate near-term (≈2yr) corporate bankruptcy risk from balance-sheet and earnings ratios.
plain_english_explanation: Weighted blend of working-capital, retained-earnings, EBIT, market-equity-to-liabilities, and sales ratios. Higher = safer. Distinct weightings exist per entity type (original manufacturing, private, and non-manufacturing/service Z'').
interpretation: Original model: Z>2.99 safe, 1.81–2.99 grey, <1.81 distress.
sector_adjustments: Banks/insurers/REITs: model not meaningful — exclude, use sector-specific solvency.
failure_modes: Negative book equity makes X2/X4 unstable.
Financials and asset-heavy regulated entities mis-scored.
common_misuse: Applying the manufacturing formula to a SaaS/service company.
Treating Z as a precise probability.
implementation_notes: Select variant by GICS sector; return zone enum; on missing input apply sector-median imputation and lower confidence (see normalization/confidence modules).
unit_tests_required: known manufacturing example matches published Z
non-manufacturing variant uses Z'' weights and drops X5
missing input -> reduced confidence, not crash
source_notes: Altman (1968) and subsequent Z''/Z' revisions — public academic finance, original wording.

_meta.note: Example card anchoring the schema. Computed by altman_z_score() in backend/app/quant/formulas/forensic. LLM never computes this.
